title: Xenial Crumbly French Rice Cake
description: A fresh, crumbly madeleine-inspired rice cake made in a cast-iron skillet, perfect for a tamasic-friendly Ayurvedic meal prep.
instructions: Preheat a cast-iron skillet over medium-low heat and lightly grease with coconut oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together almond flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and sea salt.
Add the cooked basmati rice to the dry mixture and stir until well coated.
In a separate bowl, combine coconut milk, maple syrup, and vanilla extract, then pour over the rice mixture.
Stir until a thick batter forms, then spoon the batter into the hot skillet in a round, madeleine-like shape.
Cover the skillet with a lid and cook for 12 minutes, or until the edges are golden and the center is set.
Remove the lid, flip the cake over, and cook for an additional 6 minutes to crisp the top.
Allow the cake to cool slightly before serving warm or at room temperature.
